caveats: a: No public six-digit wage-weighted task study.
a: Classified work is largely invisible in public evidence.
rho: Bounded judgment rather than observed adoption.
rho: Autonomous safety-critical design or control is excluded.
e: Injected count is margin-bridged from auto parts and especially uncertain for this project-based industry.
e: Establishment classification may not correspond to a transferable firm.
s5: Economy-wide demographic proxy is weak.
s5: Tiny denominator makes probabilities unstable.
q: No public contract sample quantifies automation pass-through.
q: Fixed-price commercial-space programs differ from cost-type defense development.
d5: Budget authority is not delivered quantity.
d5: Demand is program-concentrated and politically sensitive.
o: Operator share may migrate to large primes.
o: This is distinct from internal labor automation.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Long-cycle government and prime contracts plus commercial-space programs, with recurring development, production lots, testing, configuration, compliance, and sustainment work.
lens.excluded_activities: Parts, propulsion units, and auxiliary equipment classified elsewhere; captive divisions, government arsenals, shells, non-control financing vehicles, and firms outside the EBITDA band.
lens.included_activities: Independent LMM manufacturers and prototype-to-production developers of complete guided missiles or space vehicles that repeatedly supply external government, prime-contractor, or commercial-space customers.

a.bridge: BLS measures the broader aerospace industry, where aircraft structural and systems assemblers are 5.00% of employment and industrial engineering is prominent, rather than six-digit missile and space-vehicle tasks. I map requirements analysis, simulation support, documentation, configuration, scheduling, procurement, test-data analysis, and proposal work as exposed; classified engineering judgment, energetic integration, precision assembly, physical test, inspection, and mission assurance are less substitutable. The broader bridge may understate engineering-document exposure but overstate implementability under security constraints.
caveats: No public six-digit wage-weighted task study.
Classified work is largely invisible in public evidence.
a.change_evidence: Cleared payroll-weighted task studies across program management, engineering, software, mission assurance, test, production, and security at eligible firms would materially revise the estimate.
a.rationale: Exceptionally high labor intensity and dense engineering and program documentation create exposure, while safety, security, and physical integration cap substitution.

d5.bridge: DoD FY2026 material prioritizes missile defense, munitions, and the defense industrial base and identifies $40.2 billion requested for missile defeat and defense, while FAA forecasts rising commercial-space cadence. These are budget and activity indicators, not constant-price five-year output for eligible LMM manufacturers. I translate them into strong but volatile demand growth; appropriations, program cancellations, and prime concentration can cause either-direction error.
caveats: Budget authority is not delivered quantity.
Demand is program-concentrated and politically sensitive.
d5.change_evidence: Five-year funded procurement quantities, selected-acquisition reports, prime backlogs, commercial launch manifests, satellite orders, and eligible-firm contract pipelines would materially revise the range.
d5.rationale: Missile replenishment, missile defense, space architectures, and commercial launch activity support growth, tempered by procurement timing, technical failures, and budget volatility.

caveats: Injected count is margin-bridged from auto parts and especially uncertain for this project-based industry.
Establishment classification may not correspond to a transferable firm.
e.change_evidence: Firm-level review of all four candidates using SAM.gov, CAGE, facility clearances, contract awards, ownership, backlog, and audited EBITDA would determine eligibility.
e.rationale: The injected population is only four and likely includes large-prime establishments, strategic subsidiaries, development-stage ventures, or entities without repeat transferable production economics. A genuinely independent LMM prime with recurring contracts, clearances, and facilities is uncommon.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 336414 ($12,575,083,966) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 336414 ($19,492,991,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.0904 (margins.json[336]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Auto Parts EBITDA/Sales 9.04%); revenue/employee $539,867 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 336414); class <5: 10 firms, avg revenue/firm $507,500 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $45,878 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 3 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,467,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $132,617 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 3 firms, avg revenue/firm $4,816,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $435,366 -> out of $1-10M band; class 100-499: 4 firms, avg revenue/firm $63,985,250 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,784,267 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 10 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,919,156,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $173,491,702 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 4

caveats: Operator share may migrate to large primes.
This is distinct from internal labor automation.
o.change_evidence: Evidence of government in-house production, prime insourcing, fully outsourced digital design without independent vehicle operators, or structurally different procurement models would move the estimate.
o.rationale: Retained demand requires an accountable cleared manufacturer to integrate, test, document, and deliver physical missiles or space vehicles. Software is embedded in the product and workflow but cannot replace responsible production.

caveats: No public contract sample quantifies automation pass-through.
Fixed-price commercial-space programs differ from cost-type defense development.
q.change_evidence: Program contracts, contract type, certified cost and pricing data, negotiated fee histories, learning-curve clauses, and customer interviews would directly test retention.
q.rationale: Unique designs, security and qualification barriers, mission criticality, and long programs protect benefits. Cost-plus accounting, negotiated government pricing, audits, follow-on competition, and prime-contractor cost-down demands pass some savings through.

caveats: Bounded judgment rather than observed adoption.
Autonomous safety-critical design or control is excluded.
rho.change_evidence: Accredited pilots in secure environments measuring labor hours, defects, review burden, and approval cycle time across non-safety-critical workflows would move the range.
rho.rationale: Controlled tools can assist search, documentation, proposal, schedule, simulation, and test-data workflows within five years. Classified environments, export controls, model validation, cyber accreditation, mission assurance, sparse data, and catastrophic failure risk sharply constrain implementation.

s5.bridge: Federal Reserve owner-age data describe small businesses generally, not national-security aerospace transfers. I apply a large discount for the tiny population, strategic ownership, government consent, clearances, export controls, classified contracts, development risk, and internal restructurings. Strategic acquisitions can occur, but many are outside the LMM lens.
caveats: Economy-wide demographic proxy is weak.
Tiny denominator makes probabilities unstable.
s5.change_evidence: CAGE and SAM ownership histories, classified-contract novation records where obtainable, CFIUS disclosures, and aerospace-defense M&A data since 2015 would move the estimate.
s5.rationale: Control changes are possible through strategic acquisition, but qualifying independent firms are extremely scarce and transactions require extensive government and customer diligence.

principal_driver: Defense replenishment and expanding space architectures support strong physical demand and high-value engineering workflows.
principal_weakness: The eligible LMM acquisition universe is effectively tiny and heavily constrained by national-security requirements.

run_meta.title: Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Manufacturing

sources.supports: Census defines 336414 as manufacturing or prototyping complete guided missiles and space vehicles.
sources.title: 2022 NAICS Definition: Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Manufacturing

sources.supports: BLS reports aircraft structural, surface, rigging, and systems assemblers at 5.00% of broader aerospace employment, supporting the physical-work proxy.
sources.title: Aerospace Product and Parts Manufacturing OEWS

sources.supports: DoD states FY2026 priorities include missile defense, munitions, and the defense industrial base.

sources.supports: CRS reports the FY2026 request included $40.2 billion for missile defeat and defense procurement and RDT&E.
sources.title: FY2026 Defense Budget: Funding for Selected Weapon Systems

sources.supports: FAA describes a new era of commercial space transportation and launch-cadence expansion, used as a space-demand proxy.
sources.title: FAA Aerospace Forecast FY 2026-2046

sources.supports: Federal Reserve owner-age evidence used only as a broad succession proxy.
sources.title: 2024 Chartbook on Firms by Age of Primary Owner

title: Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Manufacturing
